title: Recursive formulas for arithmetic sequences
description: Find the recursive formula of an arithmetic sequence given the first few terms.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Complete the recursive formula of the arithmetic sequence $20, 26, 32, 38,...$.**

$a(1)=$  [[☃ numeric-input 1]]

$a(n)=a(n-1)+$ [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
hints:
The first term is $20$ and the common difference is $6$.

| $\footnotesize\maroonC{+6\,\Large\curvearrowright}$ | | $\footnotesize\maroonC{+6\,\Large\curvearrowright}$ | | $\footnotesize\maroonC{+6\,\Large\curvearrowright}$ | |
:-: |  :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$20,$ | | $26,$ | | $32,$ | | $38,...$

This is the recursive formula of $20, 26, 32, 38,...$

$\begin{cases}
a(1)=20
\\\\
a(n)=a(n-1)+6
\end{cases}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Complete the recursive formula of the arithmetic sequence $-3, -1, 1, 3,...$.**

$b(1)=$  [[☃ numeric-input 1]]

$b(n)=b(n-1)+$ [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
hints:
The first term is $-3$ and the common difference is $2$.

| $\footnotesize\maroonC{+2\,\Large\curvearrowright}$ | | $\footnotesize\maroonC{+2\,\Large\curvearrowright}$ | | $\footnotesize\maroonC{+2\,\Large\curvearrowright}$ | |
:-: |  :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$-3,$ | | $-1,$ | | $1,$ | | $3,...$

This is the recursive formula of $-3, -1, 1, 3,...$

$\begin{cases}
b(1)=-3
\\\\
b(n)=b(n-1)+2
\end{cases}$
